name: app.R
content:
# ============================================================================= # Gov 2001 -- Lecture 14b Companion Shiny App # Variance-Weighted Regression: see ATE vs OLS update in real time # # Move the sliders. Watch the bar chart, the scatter, and the gap update. # Try setting tau_0 = tau_1 (gap closes). Try p_0 = p_1 (gap closes). # Simulate to verify the three paths (OLS, FWL, sum w_g * tau_g) agree. # ============================================================================= library(shiny) library(ggplot2) # ---------- Color palette (matches the deck) ---------- HARVARDCRIMSON <- "#A51C30" SLATE <- "#4A5568" CHARCOAL <- "#2D3748" FOREST <- "#276749" OCEAN <- "#2B6CB0" WARMGRAY <- "#718096" LIGHTGRAY <- "#F7FAFC" GOLD <- "#B7791F" # Common ggplot theme deck_theme <- function(base_size = 13) { theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) + theme( plot.title = element_text(color = CHARCOAL, face = "bold"), plot.subtitle = element_text(color = SLATE), axis.title = element_text(color = SLATE), axis.text = element_text(color = SLATE), legend.title = element_text(color = SLATE), legend.text = element_text(color = SLATE), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#E2E8F0", linewidth = 0.3) ) } # ============================================================================= # UI # ============================================================================= ui <- fluidPage( withMathJax(), # Custom CSS to match the deck aesthetic tags$head( tags$style(HTML(sprintf(" body { background-color: %s; color: %s; font-family: 'Linux Libertine', Georgia, serif; } h1, h2, h3, h4 { color: %s; } .well { background-color: white; border: 1px solid #E2E8F0; } .live-numbers { background-color: %s; border-left: 4px solid %s; padding: 12px; margin-top: 10px; font-family: 'Inconsolata', monospace; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.6; } .nav-tabs > li > a { color: %s; } .nav-tabs > li.active > a { color: %s; font-weight: bold; } .formula-card { background-color: white; padding: 14px 18px; border: 1px solid #E2E8F0; border-left: 4px solid %s; border-radius: 4px; margin: 14px 0; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.5; } .formula-card p { margin: 4px 0; color: %s; } .formula-card .label { color: %s; font-weight: bold; } ", LIGHTGRAY, SLATE, CHARCOAL, "#FFF8F0", HARVARDCRIMSON, SLATE, HARVARDCRIMSON, FOREST, SLATE, CHARCOAL ))) ), titlePanel( div( h2("Variance-Weighted Regression", style = sprintf("color: %s; margin-bottom: 4px;", CHARCOAL)), h4("ATE vs. OLS in real time --- companion to Gov 2001 Lecture 14b", style = sprintf("color: %s; margin-top: 0;", WARMGRAY)) ) ), sidebarLayout( # ----------------- Sidebar: sliders + live numbers ----------------- sidebarPanel( width = 4, h4("Population parameters"), sliderInput("muG", HTML("P(G = 1)"), min = 0.05, max = 0.95, value = 0.40, step = 0.05), sliderInput("p0", HTML("p0 = P(D=1 | G=0)"), min = 0.05, max = 0.95, value = 0.10, step = 0.05), sliderInput("p1", HTML("p1 = P(D=1 | G=1)"), min = 0.05, max = 0.95, value = 0.50, step = 0.05), sliderInput("tau0", HTML("τ0 (effect in G=0)"), min = -5, max = 10, value = 2, step = 0.5), sliderInput("tau1", HTML("τ1 (effect in G=1)"), min = -5, max = 10, value = 5, step = 0.5), div(class = "live-numbers", uiOutput("live_numbers")), hr(), h5("Try this:", style = sprintf("color: %s;", CHARCOAL)), tags$ul( tags$li(em("Set "), HTML("τ0 = τ1"), em(": the gap closes (constant treatment effect).")), tags$li(em("Set "), HTML("p0 = p1"), em(": the gap closes (constant take-up).")), tags$li(em("Push "), HTML("p1"), em(" toward 0.5 with a small "), HTML("P(G=1)"), em(": the small stratum dominates the OLS weight.")) ) ), # ----------------- Main panel: tabs ----------------- mainPanel( width = 8, tabsetPanel( type = "tabs", # --- Tab 1: Weights --- tabPanel( "Weights", uiOutput("formula_card_weights"), plotOutput("weight_bars", height = "260px"), plotOutput("bernoulli_curve", height = "260px") ), # --- Tab 2: Scatter --- tabPanel( "Scatter", uiOutput("formula_card_scatter"), plotOutput("scatter", height = "480px"), p(em("Within-stratum slopes (solid). Pooled OLS slope (dashed forest). "), em("ATE slope (dotted gold). The visual gap between the two non-solid lines is OLS − ATE.")) ), # --- Tab 3: Simulate --- tabPanel( "Simulate", uiOutput("formula_card_sim"), fluidRow( column(4, numericInput("n_sim", "Sample size n:", value = 5000, min = 200, max = 100000, step = 500), actionButton("simulate", "Run simulation", class = "btn-primary") ), column(8, p(strong("Three paths to the same number:")), tags$ol( tags$li("Direct OLS: ", code("lm(Y ~ D + G)")), tags$li("FWL by hand: residualize D and Y on G, then regress"), tags$li("Variance-weighted formula on sample analogs") ) ) ), hr(), uiOutput("sim_output"), plotOutput("sim_plot", height = "320px") ) ) ) ) ) # ============================================================================= # Server # ============================================================================= server <- function(input, output, session) { # ------------- Reactive: weights and estimands (analytic) ------------- weights <- reactive({ muG <- input$muG p0 <- input$p0 p1 <- input$p1 pop_w <- c(`G=0` = 1 - muG, `G=1` = muG) bern_v <- c(`G=0` = p0 * (1 - p0), `G=1` = p1 * (1 - p1)) num <- pop_w * bern_v var_w <- num / sum(num) list(pop = pop_w, bern = bern_v, var = var_w) }) estimands <- reactive({ w <- weights() tau <- c(`G=0` = input$tau0, `G=1` = input$tau1) ate <- sum(w$pop * tau) ols <- sum(w$var * tau) list(ate = ate, ols = ols, gap = ols - ate) }) # ------------- Live numbers panel ------------- output$live_numbers <- renderUI({ e <- estimands() w <- weights() HTML(sprintf( paste( "ATE (population-weighted): %.3f
", "OLS (variance-weighted): %.3f
", "Gap (OLS − ATE): %+.3f

", "P(G=0), P(G=1): %.2f, %.2f
", "w0, w1: %.2f, %.2f", sep = ""), OCEAN, e$ate, HARVARDCRIMSON, e$ols, ifelse(abs(e$gap) < 1e-6, SLATE, FOREST), e$gap, w$pop[1], w$pop[2], w$var[1], w$var[2] )) }) # ------------- Formula card: Weights tab ------------- output$formula_card_weights <- renderUI({ e <- estimands() w <- weights() withMathJax(HTML(sprintf( '

The weight on stratum g

$$w_g \\;=\\; \\frac{P(G=g)\\,p_g(1-p_g)}{\\sum_{g\'} P(G=g\')\\,p_{g\'}(1-p_{g\'})}$$

With current sliders:

$$w_0 = \\frac{%.2f \\cdot %.3f}{%.2f \\cdot %.3f \\,+\\, %.2f \\cdot %.3f} = \\frac{%.4f}{%.4f} = \\mathbf{%.3f}$$ $$w_1 = \\frac{%.2f \\cdot %.3f}{%.2f \\cdot %.3f \\,+\\, %.2f \\cdot %.3f} = \\frac{%.4f}{%.4f} = \\mathbf{%.3f}$$
', w$pop[1], w$bern[1], w$pop[1], w$bern[1], w$pop[2], w$bern[2], w$pop[1]*w$bern[1], sum(w$pop * w$bern), w$var[1], w$pop[2], w$bern[2], w$pop[1], w$bern[1], w$pop[2], w$bern[2], w$pop[2]*w$bern[2], sum(w$pop * w$bern), w$var[2] ))) }) # ------------- Formula card: Scatter tab ------------- output$formula_card_scatter <- renderUI({ e <- estimands() w <- weights() withMathJax(HTML(sprintf( '

ATE (population-weighted):

$$\\text{ATE} \\;=\\; \\sum_g P(G=g)\\,\\tau_g \\;=\\; %.2f \\cdot %g \\,+\\, %.2f \\cdot %g \\;=\\; \\mathbf{%.2f}$$

OLS (variance-weighted):

$$\\beta_1^{\\text{OLS}} \\;=\\; \\sum_g w_g\\,\\tau_g \\;=\\; %.3f \\cdot %g \\,+\\, %.3f \\cdot %g \\;=\\; \\mathbf{%.2f}$$

Gap: $\\;\\beta_1^{\\text{OLS}} - \\text{ATE} = \\mathbf{%+.2f}$

', GOLD, w$pop[1], input$tau0, w$pop[2], input$tau1, e$ate, FOREST, w$var[1], input$tau0, w$var[2], input$tau1, e$ols, HARVARDCRIMSON, e$gap ))) }) # ------------- Formula card: Simulate tab ------------- output$formula_card_sim <- renderUI({ withMathJax(HTML( '

FWL identity:

$$\\beta_1^{\\text{OLS}} \\;=\\; \\frac{\\mathrm{Cov}(\\tilde D, Y)}{\\mathrm{Var}(\\tilde D)} \\;=\\; \\sum_g w_g\\,\\tau_g$$

where $\\tilde D = D - \\mathbb{E}[D \\mid G]$ is $D$ with the part predicted by $G$ removed. The simulation below verifies all three paths land on the same number.

' )) }) # ------------- Plot 1: Weight bars ------------- output$weight_bars <- renderPlot({ w <- weights() df <- data.frame( stratum = rep(c("G = 0", "G = 1"), 2), type = factor(rep(c("Population P(G=g)", "Variance weight w_g"), each = 2), levels = c("Population P(G=g)", "Variance weight w_g")), share = c(w$pop, w$var) ) ggplot(df, aes(x = stratum, y = share, fill = type)) + geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) + geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", share)), position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), vjust = -0.5, size = 4.5, color = CHARCOAL) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("Population P(G=g)" = OCEAN, "Variance weight w_g" = HARVARDCRIMSON)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(title = "Population weights vs. variance weights", x = NULL, y = "Share", fill = NULL) + deck_theme() + theme(legend.position = "top") }) # ------------- Plot 2: Bernoulli curve ------------- output$bernoulli_curve <- renderPlot({ p_grid <- seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01) df_curve <- data.frame(p = p_grid, bv = p_grid * (1 - p_grid)) df_pts <- data.frame( p = c(input$p0, input$p1), bv = c(input$p0 * (1 - input$p0), input$p1 * (1 - input$p1)), label = c("p[0]", "p[1]") ) ggplot(df_curve, aes(x = p, y = bv)) + geom_line(color = FOREST, linewidth = 1.2) + geom_point(data = df_pts, aes(x = p, y = bv), size = 5, color = HARVARDCRIMSON) + geom_text(data = df_pts, aes(x = p, y = bv, label = label), parse = TRUE, vjust = -1.4, hjust = 0.5, size = 5, color = HARVARDCRIMSON) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.30), expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(title = "Bernoulli variance curve: p(1 - p)", subtitle = "Strata where take-up is closer to 0.5 carry more weight", x = "p (take-up rate within stratum)", y = "p(1 - p)") + deck_theme() }) # ------------- Plot 3: Scatter with within-stratum and pooled lines ------------- scatter_data <- reactive({ # A small, deterministic scatter for visual texture set.seed(42) n_per <- 30 G <- rep(c(0, 1), each = 2 * n_per) pG <- ifelse(G == 1, input$p1, input$p0) D <- rbinom(length(G), 1, pG) tauG <- ifelse(G == 1, input$tau1, input$tau0) Y <- D * tauG + rnorm(length(G), 0, 0.5) data.frame(D = D, Y = Y, G = factor(G, labels = c("G = 0", "G = 1"))) }) output$scatter <- renderPlot({ df <- scatter_data() e <- estimands() # Pooled OLS line: anchor at within-stratum-D=0 means Y_at_D0 <- mean(df$Y[df$D == 0]) # Endpoint y-values at x = 1 for label placement y_g0 <- input$tau0 # blue G=0 line y_g1 <- input$tau1 # red G=1 line y_ols <- Y_at_D0 + e$ols # forest dashed y_ate <- Y_at_D0 + e$ate # gold dotted label_x <- 1.04 label_df <- data.frame( x = label_x, y = c(y_g0, y_g1, y_ols, y_ate), lab = c( sprintf("G=0 slope = %g", input$tau0), sprintf("G=1 slope = %g", input$tau1), sprintf("OLS = %.2f", e$ols), sprintf("ATE = %.2f", e$ate) ), col = c(OCEAN, HARVARDCRIMSON, FOREST, GOLD), bold = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE), stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ggplot(df, aes(x = D, y = Y, color = G)) + geom_jitter(width = 0.04, height = 0.05, size = 2.2, alpha = 0.7) + # Within-stratum lines (solid, colored by group) geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2, formula = y ~ x) + # ATE line (gold, dotted) geom_abline(intercept = Y_at_D0, slope = e$ate, color = GOLD, linewidth = 1.3, linetype = "dotted") + # OLS line (forest, dashed) geom_abline(intercept = Y_at_D0, slope = e$ols, color = FOREST, linewidth = 1.4, linetype = "dashed") + # End-of-line labels with white backgrounds in clear space geom_label(data = label_df[!label_df$bold, ], aes(x = x, y = y, label = lab), color = label_df$col[!label_df$bold], fill = "white", size = 3.8, hjust = 0, label.size = 0, label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"), inherit.aes = FALSE) + geom_label(data = label_df[label_df$bold, ], aes(x = x, y = y, label = lab), color = label_df$col[label_df$bold], fill = "white", size = 4.4, hjust = 0, fontface = "bold", label.size = 0, label.padding = unit(0.18, "lines"), inherit.aes = FALSE) + scale_color_manual(values = c("G = 0" = OCEAN, "G = 1" = HARVARDCRIMSON)) + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 1), limits = c(-0.1, 1.5)) + labs(title = "Within-stratum slopes, pooled OLS, and ATE --- all on one canvas", x = "D (treatment)", y = "Y (outcome)", color = NULL) + deck_theme(base_size = 14) + theme(legend.position = "none") }) # ------------- Simulation ------------- sim_results <- eventReactive(input$simulate, { n <- input$n_sim set.seed(2026) G <- rbinom(n, 1, input$muG) pG <- ifelse(G == 1, input$p1, input$p0) D <- rbinom(n, 1, pG) tauG <- ifelse(G == 1, input$tau1, input$tau0) Y <- D * tauG + rnorm(n, 0, 1) # Path 1: direct OLS fit_ols <- lm(Y ~ D + G) beta_ols <- coef(fit_ols)["D"] # Path 2: FWL by hand D_tilde <- residuals(lm(D ~ G)) Y_tilde <- residuals(lm(Y ~ G)) beta_fwl <- coef(lm(Y_tilde ~ D_tilde - 1))["D_tilde"] # Path 3: variance-weighted formula on sample analogs mu_hat <- mean(G) p_hat <- tapply(D, G, mean) prG <- c(1 - mu_hat, mu_hat) num <- prG * p_hat * (1 - p_hat) w_hat <- num / sum(num) means <- tapply(Y, list(D, G), mean) tau_hat <- means["1", ] - means["0", ] beta_vw <- sum(w_hat * tau_hat) ate_hat <- sum(prG * tau_hat) list(beta_ols = beta_ols, beta_fwl = beta_fwl, beta_vw = beta_vw, ate_hat = ate_hat, w_hat = w_hat, tau_hat = tau_hat, mu_hat = mu_hat, prG = prG, n = n) }) output$sim_output <- renderUI({ s <- sim_results() HTML(sprintf( paste( "
", "n = %d

", "Path 1 — Direct OLS: β̂ = %.4f
", "Path 2 — FWL by hand: β̂ = %.4f
", "Path 3 — Σ wg τg on sample analogs: β̂ = %.4f

", "ATE on sample analogs: %.4f
", "Gap (OLS − ATE): %+.4f", "
", sep = ""), s$n, HARVARDCRIMSON, s$beta_ols, HARVARDCRIMSON, s$beta_fwl, HARVARDCRIMSON, s$beta_vw, OCEAN, s$ate_hat, FOREST, s$beta_ols - s$ate_hat )) }) output$sim_plot <- renderPlot({ s <- sim_results() df <- data.frame( stratum = rep(c("G = 0", "G = 1"), 2), type = factor(rep(c("Population P(G=g)", "Variance weight w_g"), each = 2), levels = c("Population P(G=g)", "Variance weight w_g")), share = c(s$prG, s$w_hat) ) ggplot(df, aes(x = stratum, y = share, fill = type)) + geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) + geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", share)), position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), vjust = -0.5, size = 4.5, color = CHARCOAL) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("Population P(G=g)" = OCEAN, "Variance weight w_g" = HARVARDCRIMSON)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(title = sprintf("Sample-analog weights at n = %d", s$n), x = NULL, y = "Share", fill = NULL) + deck_theme() + theme(legend.position = "top") }) } # ============================================================================= shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

name: README.md
content:
# Variance-Weighted Regression — Companion Shiny App

This is the interactive companion to **Gov 2001 Lecture 14b** (Wednesday Apr 29). Move the sliders, watch ATE, OLS, and the gap update in real time. Verify the three-paths equivalence by simulation.

## What it shows

The app makes the variance-weighted regression formula tangible:

$$
\beta_1^{\text{OLS}} \;=\; \sum_g w_g \, \tau_g, \qquad
w_g \;=\; \frac{P(G=g)\, p_g(1-p_g)}{\sum_{g'} P(G=g')\, p_{g'}(1-p_{g'})}
$$

Five sliders control the population:

- **P(G = 1)** — share of stratum 1 in the population
- **p₀** — take-up rate within stratum 0, P(D = 1 | G = 0)
- **p₁** — take-up rate within stratum 1, P(D = 1 | G = 1)
- **τ₀** — within-stratum treatment effect for G = 0
- **τ₁** — within-stratum treatment effect for G = 1

Three views:

- **Weights tab** — bar chart of population vs. variance weights, plus the Bernoulli variance curve p(1-p) with markers at p₀ and p₁
- **Scatter tab** — two within-stratum slopes plus the pooled OLS line, with simulated jittered points for visual texture
- **Simulate tab** — run a sample of size n and verify three paths to the OLS coefficient (direct, FWL by hand, Σ wₘ τₘ on sample analogs) all agree

The sidebar always shows live-updating numbers: ATE, OLS, gap, and the weights themselves.

## Pedagogical exercises

1. **Match the deck.** With defaults (P(G=1)=0.4, p₀=0.1, p₁=0.5, τ₀=2, τ₁=5), verify ATE ≈ 3.20 and OLS ≈ 3.95.
2. **Constant treatment effect.** Set τ₀ = τ₁. The gap should close to zero.
3. **Constant take-up.** Set p₀ = p₁. The variance weights collapse to the population weights, so OLS = ATE.
4. **Słoczyński's pathological case.** Set P(G=1) = 0.1, p₀ = 0.95, p₁ = 0.5. Notice that the small minority stratum carries most of the OLS weight. If τ₀ ≠ τ₁, the OLS coefficient is far from any sensible average.
5. **Where the gap maxes out.** Push p₁ toward 0.5 and pull p₀ toward 0 or 1, with τ values that differ. Observe how the gap grows.

## How to run

### Requirements

- R (≥ 4.0)
- Packages: `shiny`, `ggplot2`

If you don't already have them:

```r
install.packages(c("shiny", "ggplot2"))
```

### Launch from R / RStudio

```r
shiny::runApp("path/to/14b_variance_weights")
```

### Launch from the command line

```bash
cd path/to/14b_variance_weights
Rscript -e 'shiny::runApp(".", launch.browser = TRUE)'
```

The app will open in your default browser at a local address such as `http://127.0.0.1:XXXX`.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app.R`   | The Shiny application (single-file form) |
| `README.md` | This file |

## Connection to the lecture

The app is a direct companion to **Lecture 14b: Variance-Weighted Regression**, which derives the formula above from FWL plus iterated expectations (Angrist 1998). It also serves as preparation for **Final Exam Problem 5** (Practice Problem 5c), which asks students to derive the variance-weighted decomposition by hand on binary D and binary G.

Slide 33 of `14b_variance_weights.pdf` shows the connection explicitly.
